calls.note: For the long term it can be held. A strong correction has been seen, but on a last-10-years basis Trent is an outperformer. A long-term investor can definitely hold and add on dips - it's a lifestyle stock.

The disinvestment news keeps flip-flopping - it will come, it won't come. Only once the disinvestment news comes completely and concretely should one take a position; otherwise better to avoid in the medium to short term.
Not a great result - only modest top-line growth, though net profit jumped 200%. But one can't judge from a single quarter's result; wait and look at the Q4 numbers before deciding.
